# Test the bulk `nlm add` CLI surface added in cmd/nlm/add_sources.go.
# These exercises validate argument-count handling before any network call.
# Content-level validation (missing-file fail-fast, mixed-stdin rejection)
# runs inside run() after auth is verified; those paths are covered by the
# unit test TestValidateSourceInputs rather than txtar.

# `nlm add` with no arguments — bad-args exit 2 with usage hint.
! exec ./nlm_test add
stderr 'usage: nlm add <notebook-id> <source\|-> \[source\.\.\.\]'
! stderr 'panic'

# `nlm add` with only a notebook id — same usage hint, still bad-args.
! exec ./nlm_test add notebook123
stderr 'usage: nlm add <notebook-id> <source\|-> \[source\.\.\.\]'
! stderr 'panic'

# Two inputs after the notebook id pass argument-count validation and
# then fall through to the auth check (evidence the maxArgs=-1 change
# took effect — prior code would have rejected 3+ positional args).
! exec ./nlm_test add notebook123 https://a/x https://b/y
stderr 'Authentication required'
! stderr 'usage: nlm add'
! stderr 'panic'
